# go-production-reachability — installed for the ddx project itself.
# Source: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml
#
# This project opts in to the Go reachability check: every bead-merge that
# touches *.go files runs the check across the cli/ Go module, ensuring new
# functions/methods are wired into the production call graph reachable from
# main(). Backfill of existing unwired symbols is tracked under REACH-BACKFILL
# (ddx-83440482) — until that lands, this check may surface known violations.

name: production-reachability
when: pre_merge
applies_to:
  paths:
    - "*.go"
command: |
  cd library/checks/go-production-reachability && \
    go run . \
      --module-dir=cli \
      --packages=./...

ID:       ddx-09d2990c
Title:    audit + wire (or delete) the unwired escalation/retry/triage code from recent landed beads
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, story:10, area:agent, kind:audit, observed-failure
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
Accept:   1. For each of the 4 functions: trace the bead that introduced it; verify the bead's AC still describes desired behavior (or document obsolescence). 2. For each: either wire into execute_bead_loop.go (if still relevant) OR delete the function + its tests + reopen the originating bead with the obsolescence rationale. 3. After wiring: spawn a worker, drain a few beads, verify execution evidence shows the new code paths firing. 4. Save the audit findings + decisions per function to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/wire-decisions.md. 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. The 5 originating beads (ddx-da11a34a, ddx-aede917d, ddx-cfedee8e, etc.) verified via test execution showing their behavior is now active in production.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""File one child bead per package cluster of production-reachability violations."""
import subprocess
import collections
import json
import sys
import re

DDX = "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-83440482-20260503T124553-282667f7/cli/ddx"
PARENT = "ddx-83440482"
LABELS = "phase:2,area:checks,kind:backfill"

# Group by package.
clusters = collections.OrderedDict()
with open("/tmp/deadcode-flat.tsv") as f:
    for line in f:
        pkg, file_, lineno, sym = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
        clusters.setdefault(pkg, []).append((file_, lineno, sym))

# Write summary.
created = []
for pkg, syms in clusters.items():
    short = pkg.split("github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/")[1]
    title = f"checks: backfill production-reachability — {short} ({len(syms)} unreached)"
    sym_list = "\n".join(f"- {file_}:{lineno} — {sym}" for file_, lineno, sym in syms)
    desc = (
        f"Decomposed from REACH-BACKFILL ({PARENT}). The Go production-reachability check "
        f"(library/checks/go-production-reachability) flagged {len(syms)} symbol(s) in "
        f"package `{short}` as unreachable from cli/ entry roots (deadcode RTA).\n\n"
        f"Symbols:\n{sym_list}\n\n"
        f"For each: WIRE (add to production call graph) or DELETE (genuinely obsolete). "
        f"If neither is clear within ~15 min, annotate `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` "
        f"and file a follow-up bead.\n\n"
        f"Decision rule: if the originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE; "
        f"if speculative or design has moved on → DELETE.\n\n"
        f"Initial-violations evidence: .ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/initial-violations.json"
    )
    accept = (
        f"1. Each of the {len(syms)} listed symbols is either wired into the production call graph "
        f"reachable from main() OR deleted (with originating bead reopened/closed-as-obsolete as "
        f"appropriate).\n"
        f"2. Any remaining wiring:pending annotations cite open follow-up beads.\n"
        f"3. After landing: deadcode RTA (`go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./...` "
        f"from cli/) reports zero remaining dead symbols in `{short}`.\n"
        f"4. cd cli && go test ./... green.\n"
        f"5. Decisions log written to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/decisions.md (one line per symbol: "
        f"WIRE|DELETE|PENDING <reason>)."
    )
    cmd = [
        DDX, "bead", "create", title,
        "--parent", PARENT,
        "--labels", LABELS,
        "--description", desc,
        "--acceptance", accept,
        "--type", "task",
        "--priority", "2",
    ]
    out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if out.returncode != 0:
        print(f"FAILED for {pkg}: {out.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    # ddx bead create prints the new bead id somewhere in output
    m = re.search(r"ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}", out.stdout + out.stderr)
    if not m:
        print(f"Could not find bead id in output: {out.stdout!r} / {out.stderr!r}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    bid = m.group(0)
    created.append({"id": bid, "package": short, "count": len(syms)})
    print(f"created {bid} for {short} ({len(syms)})")

with open("/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-83440482-20260503T124553-282667f7/.ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/children.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(created, f, indent=2)

Parent stays open until all children land + final ddx ac run reports zero violations." 2>&1

./cli/ddx bead update ddx-09d2990c --notes "superseded by REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482); the 4 unwired functions (escalation.Ladder.Next, EvaluateRetryPolicy, RunWithLockBackoff, DispatchPostAttempt) surfaced as initial production-reachability violations and are absorbed into child bead ddx-83b8994f (internal/agent backfill)." 2>&1

data.text: Now write the rationale and the summary.

input.content:
# REACH-BACKFILL decomposition

Bead: ddx-83440482 — checks: systematic backfill — apply production-reachability across cli/ and resolve all violations

## Why decomposed (Step 0 size check)

This bead's contract is a sweep: file children per cluster, drain them, achieve zero violations. The bead's own description prescribes the workflow ("File one child bead per violation cluster… Drain the children"). A single execute-bead attempt cannot land 283 wire/delete decisions across 21 packages and still pass the bead's AC. Per the executor instructions: "A clean decomposition is a successful attempt."

## How violations were enumerated

The installed `production-reachability` check is diff-scoped (it flags only newly-added unreached symbols in the current diff range), so a base==head ac run reports `pass — no diff range`. For backfill we need an exhaustive scan, which is exactly what the underlying engine — `golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode` (RTA) — produces. Run from `cli/`:

    go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 -json ./...

That output is preserved verbatim at `initial-violations.json` and flattened to `initial-violations.tsv` (one row per unreached symbol: `package\tfile\tline\tsymbol`).

Total unreached symbols: **283** across **21** packages.

## Cluster grouping

One child bead per Go package (sensible, matches blame boundaries, keeps each child small enough to drain in a single attempt). Per-package counts:

| Child bead | Package | Count |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ddx-0131ebf0 | cmd | 69 |
| ddx-83b8994f | internal/agent | 66 |
| ddx-895fd8bb | internal/server/perf | 17 |
| ddx-c96fc86c | internal/persona | 16 |
| ddx-a78f836f | internal/testutils | 15 |
| ddx-2850c4dc | internal/metric | 14 |
| ddx-90901b22 | internal/server | 12 |
| ddx-503c34fa | internal/git | 9 |
| ddx-2da07e5c | internal/agent/try | 9 |
| ddx-ae4b7393 | internal/evidence | 8 |
| ddx-91fe7a1a | internal/config | 8 |
| ddx-b42dd3a0 | internal/bead | 8 |
| ddx-9df0636c | internal/escalation | 6 |
| ddx-cd51f35b | internal/agent/testfixtures | 6 |
| ddx-4c5beab2 | internal/server/graphql | 5 |
| ddx-9f6baafe | internal/exec | 5 |
| ddx-abb40ce5 | internal/agent/escalation | 4 |
| ddx-7f4cdb7a | internal/update | 2 |
| ddx-8c273456 | internal/federation | 2 |
| ddx-d0d8d615 | internal/registry | 1 |
| ddx-a7fac0fc | internal/metaprompt | 1 |

Each child carries the per-package symbol list, the WIRE-or-DELETE decision rule from the parent, and a per-package AC (zero remaining unreached symbols + tests green).

Dependency edges: parent ddx-83440482 depends on all 21 children, so `ddx bead ready` will not surface the parent until every cluster lands.

## Cross-bead linkage

- **WIRE bead ddx-09d2990c** (already closed prior to this run) was annotated with a supersession note pointing at ddx-83440482 / ddx-83b8994f. Its 4 originally-cited unwired functions are confirmed still dead in the current scan and are absorbed into the `internal/agent` child:
  - `internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:93` — `Ladder.Next`
  - `internal/agent/retry_policy.go:113` — `EvaluateRetryPolicy`
  - `internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go:170` — `RunWithLockBackoff`
  - `internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go:278` — `DispatchPostAttempt`
- The Codex audit additions named in the parent description (jsonlFallbackForCollection, Store.breakStaleLock, NewStoreWithBackend, WithSpokeHTTPClient, Server.execStore, GraphQL NewResolver) are subsumed by the per-package children for `internal/bead`, `internal/federation`, `internal/server`, and `internal/server/graphql`.

## What still has to happen for the parent to close

1. Each of the 21 children drained (each lands a wire or delete change for the symbols it owns; tests green; per-package deadcode count = 0 for that package).
2. Final `ddx ac run --check production-reachability` against the post-merge tip emits `status=pass` with no violations.
3. Final summary written to `.ddx/executions/<final-run-id>/reach-backfill-summary.md` with the AC#7 fields (total / wired / deleted / follow-ups / LOC delta).

That final summary belongs to the last run that closes the parent — not this attempt.

status: needs_investigation
reason: Bead ddx-83440482 is a sweep that mandates filing one child bead per violation cluster and draining each child via wire-or-delete PRs (its own description spells out that workflow). A single execute-bead attempt cannot legitimately satisfy AC#3/#5/#7 (each child landed; final ddx ac run reports zero violations across cli/; final summary report) because those require many independent code changes across 21 packages. Per executor Step 0, this attempt performed the decomposition only — not implementation — which the harness explicitly recognises as a successful attempt.

What this attempt produced (no commit):
  - Captured the exhaustive reachability violation set via deadcode RTA (the installed production-reachability check is diff-scoped, so a base==head run is a no-op; deadcode is the same engine the check delegates to). Output saved at:
      .ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/initial-violations.json   (raw deadcode -json, 283 unreached symbols)
      .ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/initial-violations.tsv   (flattened: package\tfile\tline\tsymbol)
  - Filed 21 per-package child beads (ddx-0131ebf0, ddx-83b8994f, ddx-abb40ce5, ddx-cd51f35b, ddx-2da07e5c, ddx-b42dd3a0, ddx-91fe7a1a, ddx-9df0636c, ddx-ae4b7393, ddx-9f6baafe, ddx-8c273456, ddx-503c34fa, ddx-a7fac0fc, ddx-2850c4dc, ddx-c96fc86c, ddx-d0d8d615, ddx-90901b22, ddx-4c5beab2, ddx-895fd8bb, ddx-a78f836f, ddx-7f4cdb7a) — see children.json and decomposition.md in this evidence dir.
  - Added dep edges: parent ddx-83440482 depends on each child, so ddx bead ready will not surface the parent until every child closes.
  - Updated parent bead notes with the decomposition map.
  - Annotated already-closed WIRE bead ddx-09d2990c with a supersession note pointing at ddx-83440482 / ddx-83b8994f. All 4 of its originally-cited unwired functions are confirmed still dead in the scan and are absorbed by the internal/agent child.

Why no commit:
  - Decomposition wrote only to bead state (.ddx/beads.jsonl) and evidence files; the bead state is owned by the bead store, not committed in the worktree, and the evidence files live under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ which is preserved by the harness. Per Step 0, "A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition." Touching cli/ source in the same attempt would violate that.

What a follow-up needs:
  - Drain the 21 children (each is a focused wire-or-delete pass on one Go package; each child's AC is per-package zero-violation + tests green).
  - Once every child closes, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the post-merge tip and confirm status=pass with no violations; write reach-backfill-summary.md (AC#7) and close ddx-83440482.

data.text:
Decomposition complete. Summary:

- **Captured** 283 unreached Go symbols across 21 packages via deadcode RTA into `.ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/initial-violations.{json,tsv}`. The installed `production-reachability` check is diff-scoped so base==head is a no-op; deadcode is the same engine it delegates to.
- **Filed 21 per-package child beads** (one per Go package) with the WIRE-or-DELETE decision rule and per-package zero-violation AC. List in `children.json`; full rationale in `decomposition.md`.
- **Added dep edges** so parent `ddx-83440482` is blocked by all 21 children.
- **Updated parent bead notes** with the decomposition map.
- **Annotated WIRE bead `ddx-09d2990c`** (already closed) as superseded; its 4 unwired functions are confirmed still dead and absorbed into child `ddx-83b8994f` (internal/agent).
- **No commit** per Step 0 rule that decomposition is a clean attempt and must not be mixed with implementation. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `status: needs_investigation`.
